title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
code: library(mgcv) #> Loading required package: nlme #> This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'. library(circlss)  # von Mises deviates, Best & Fisher (1979) rejection sampling rvm <- function(n, mu, kappa) {   mu <- rep_len(mu, n); kappa <- rep_len(kappa, n); out <- numeric(n)   for (i in seq_len(n)) {     k <- kappa[i]     a <- 1 + sqrt(1 + 4 * k * k); b <- (a - sqrt(2 * a)) / (2 * k)     r <- (1 + b * b) / (2 * b)     repeat {       z <- cos(pi * runif(1)); f <- (1 + r * z) / (r + z)       cc <- k * (r - f); u2 <- runif(1)       if (cc * (2 - cc) - u2 > 0 || log(cc / u2) + 1 - cc >= 0) {         out[i] <- sign(runif(1) - 0.5) * acos(max(min(f, 1), -1)) + mu[i]         break       }     }   }   atan2(sin(out), cos(out)) }  set.seed(20260612) n <- 200 phi <- runif(n, -pi, pi) mu_true <- 2 * atan(1.6 * sin(phi)) kappa_true <- exp(1.4 + 0.6 * cos(phi)) dat <- data.frame(theta = rvm(n, mu_true, kappa_true), phi = phi)

title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: distribution parameters get cyclic smooth; knots pin period (−π,π](-\pi, \pi]. Smoothing parameters selected full Newton REML (family implements log-likelihood derivatives fourth order). Everything mgcv provides general families works usual — two estimated smooths, link scale credible bands (η1\eta_1: tan-half location; η2\eta_2: log concentration):
code: b <- gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10),                     ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10)),          family = vmlss(), data = dat, method = "REML",          knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi))) summary(b) #>  #> Family: vmlss  #> Link function: tanhalf log  #>  #> Formula: #> theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10) #> ~s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10) #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)   0.008304   0.050229   0.165    0.869     #> (Intercept).1 1.257866   0.091100  13.808   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>            edf Ref.df Chi.sq p-value     #> s(phi)   5.563      8 511.37  <2e-16 *** #> s.1(phi) 2.893      8  34.23  <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 62.2% #> -REML = 201.38  Scale est. = 1         n = 200 plot(b, pages = 1, scheme = 1)

title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: fitted mean direction μ̂(φ)\hat\mu(\varphi) response scale, data. link mean stays inside (−π,π)(-\pi, \pi), Fisher–Lee branch: 95% band comes link-scale standard errors (mgcv’s Bayesian Vp intervals) pushed monotone tan-half link, standard endpoint transform:  Remember axes circles: top bottom edges line, left right — exactly torus shows without cut.
code: phig <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 400) prl <- predict(b, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), type = "link",                se.fit = TRUE) mu_hat <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1]) mu_lo <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1] - 1.96 * prl$se.fit[, 1]) mu_hi <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1] + 1.96 * prl$se.fit[, 1])  op <- par(mar = c(4, 4, 1, 1)) plot(dat$phi, dat$theta, pch = 19, col = adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", 0.5),      cex = 0.6, xlab = expression(varphi ~ "(predictor angle)"),      ylab = expression(theta ~ "(response angle)"),      xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-pi, pi), axes = FALSE) axis(1, at = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),      labels = expression(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi)) axis(2, at = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),      labels = expression(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi)) box() polygon(c(phig, rev(phig)), c(mu_lo, rev(mu_hi)), border = NA,         col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", 0.15)) lines(phig, 2 * atan(1.6 * sin(phig)), col = "gray60", lwd = 1.6, lty = 2) lines(phig, mu_hat, col = "#c0392b", lwd = 2.6) legend("topleft", bty = "n", lwd = c(2.6, 1.6), lty = c(1, 2),        col = c("#c0392b", "gray60"),        legend = c(expression(hat(mu)(varphi) %+-% "95% band"), "truth")) par(op)

title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: Predictor angle φ\varphi around ring, response angle θ\theta around tube; (φ,θ)(\varphi, \theta) maps ((R+rcos⁡θ)cos⁡φ,(R+rcos⁡θ)sin⁡φ,rsin⁡θ)\big((R + r\cos\theta)\cos\varphi,\ (R + r\cos\theta)\sin\varphi,\ r\sin\theta\big). Base graphics enough: persp() supplies projection matrix everything drawn trans3d(), depth-based fading 3-D cue (dashed circle outer equator θ=0\theta = 0, zero-response reference).  red curve fitted conditional mean direction μ̂(φ)\hat\mu(\varphi) 95% ribbon: closed loop torus, rising tube response leads predictor dipping lags, crossing dashed equator two angles μ=0\mu = 0. ribbon widens data thin .
code: torus_xyz <- function(phi, theta, R = 1.9, r = 0.95) {   list(x = (R + r * cos(theta)) * cos(phi),        y = (R + r * cos(theta)) * sin(phi),        z = r * sin(theta)) }  # view-space depth from the persp transformation matrix (trans3d gives # only the projected x/y; the z/w component orders front vs back) depth3d <- function(x, y, z, pm) {   p <- cbind(x, y, z, 1) %*% pm   p[, 3] / p[, 4] }  draw_torus <- function(dat, mu_hat, phig, lo = NULL, hi = NULL,                        R = 1.9, r = 0.95, theta_view = 45, phi_view = 38) {   op <- par(mar = c(0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 0.2))   on.exit(par(op))   lim <- R + r + 0.1   pm <- persp(x = c(-lim, lim), y = c(-lim, lim),               z = matrix(c(-r, -r, r, r), 2, 2),               zlim = c(-r - 0.5, r + 0.5),               theta = theta_view, phi = phi_view, d = 4,               scale = FALSE, expand = 1,               col = NA, border = NA, box = FALSE, axes = FALSE)    ## wireframe: tube circles at fixed phi, ring circles at fixed theta   thd <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 80)   for (p in seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 49)[-1]) {     w <- torus_xyz(rep(p, length(thd)), thd, R, r)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }   phd <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 160)   for (t in seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 25)[-1]) {     w <- torus_xyz(phd, rep(t, length(phd)), R, r)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }    ## outer equator (theta = 0): the zero-response reference line   w <- torus_xyz(phd, rep(0, length(phd)), R, r)   lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray55", lty = 3, lwd = 0.9)    ## data on the torus surface, depth-faded (near = opaque, far = faint)   w <- torus_xyz(dat$phi, dat$theta, R, r)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.15 + 0.7 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   pt <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   ord <- order(dp)  # paint far points first   points(pt$x[ord], pt$y[ord], pch = 19,          cex = 0.4 + 0.25 * a[ord],          col = sapply(a[ord], function(ai) adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", ai)))    ## 95% ribbon between lo(phi) and hi(phi): translucent quads painted in   ## depth order, subdivided across the band so it follows the tube   if (!is.null(lo) && !is.null(hi)) {     K <- 4     i0 <- seq_len(length(phig) - 1)     quads <- list()     for (k in seq_len(K)) {       t0 <- lo + (hi - lo) * (k - 1) / K       t1 <- lo + (hi - lo) * k / K       for (i in i0) {         th <- c(t0[i], t0[i + 1], t1[i + 1], t1[i])         ph <- c(phig[i], phig[i + 1], phig[i + 1], phig[i])         w <- torus_xyz(ph, th, R, r)         quads[[length(quads) + 1]] <-           list(p = trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm),                d = mean(depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)))       }     }     dq <- vapply(quads, `[[`, numeric(1), "d")     aq <- 0.05 + 0.13 * (dq - min(dq)) / diff(range(dq))     for (j in order(dq)) {       polygon(quads[[j]]$p$x, quads[[j]]$p$y, border = NA,               col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", aq[j]))     }   }    ## fitted mean-direction curve mu-hat(phi), winding over the tube --   ## painted in depth order, far half faint   w <- torus_xyz(phig, mu_hat, R, r)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   cv <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.25 + 0.75 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   seg <- data.frame(x0 = head(cv$x, -1), y0 = head(cv$y, -1),                     x1 = tail(cv$x, -1), y1 = tail(cv$y, -1),                     d = head(dp, -1), a = head(a, -1))   seg <- seg[order(seg$d), ]   segments(seg$x0, seg$y0, seg$x1, seg$y1,            col = sapply(seg$a, function(ai) adjustcolor("#c0392b", ai)),            lwd = 2.2 + 1.6 * seg$a, lend = 1)   invisible(pm) }  draw_torus(dat, mu_hat, phig, lo = mu_lo, hi = mu_hi)

what: What this model can and cannot represent
title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: scope note trusting C–C data. vmlss mean map μ(φ)=2arctan⁡{η(φ)}\mu(\varphi) = 2\arctan\{\eta(\varphi)\} η\eta continuous periodic, fitted curve () can never touch antipode θ=±π\theta = \pm\pi response’s coordinate origin, (ii) winding number zero — φ\varphi travels around ring, curve must come back without wrapping tube. oscillation exactly class. excludes rotation-type association, θ≈φ\theta \approx \varphi (wind direction tt vs t+Δt + \Delta, paired /headings), response winds per cycle predictor. case belongs projected normal family pnlss(): response angle bivariate normal mean (μ1(φ),μ2(φ))(\mu_1(\varphi), \mu_2(\varphi)), components identity-linked, fitted direction atan2(μ̂2,μ̂1)\mathrm{atan2}(\hat\mu_2, \hat\mu_1) branch cut winds freely:  fitted curve now winds around tube going around ring — (1,1) curve torus, shape vmlss produce. (band delta-method interval: direction depends linear predictors, joint posterior covariance pushed atan2(μ2,μ1)\mathrm{atan2}(\mu_2, \mu_1) — two lines lpmatrix algebra, taste inference works parameters entangled.) (vmlss can still handle known winding mm modelling wrapped residual θ−mφ\theta - m\varphi adding mφm\varphi back; pnlss just makes detour unnecessary.) trade-runs way : vmlss separates direction concentration interpretable parameters smooths, pnlss entangles (μ1,μ2)(\mu_1, \mu_2).
code: set.seed(2) n <- 300 phi2 <- runif(n, -pi, pi) # true direction = phi (winding number 1), constant concentration theta2 <- atan2(2 * sin(phi2) + rnorm(n), 2 * cos(phi2) + rnorm(n)) dat2 <- data.frame(theta = theta2, phi = phi2)  b2 <- gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10),                      ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10)),           family = pnlss(), data = dat2, method = "REML",           knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi)))  pr2 <- predict(b2, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), type = "response") dir_hat <- atan2(pr2[, 2], pr2[, 1])  # 95% band for the direction by the delta method: the direction is a # function of BOTH linear predictors, so propagate the joint coefficient # covariance Vp through atan2(mu2, mu1) via the lpmatrix Xp <- predict(b2, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), type = "lpmatrix") lpi <- attr(Xp, "lpi") X1 <- Xp[, lpi[[1]], drop = FALSE] X2 <- Xp[, lpi[[2]], drop = FALSE] V <- b2$Vp v11 <- rowSums((X1 %*% V[lpi[[1]], lpi[[1]]]) * X1) v22 <- rowSums((X2 %*% V[lpi[[2]], lpi[[2]]]) * X2) v12 <- rowSums((X1 %*% V[lpi[[1]], lpi[[2]]]) * X2) m1 <- pr2[, 1]; m2 <- pr2[, 2]; r2 <- m1^2 + m2^2 se_dir <- sqrt(pmax(m2^2 * v11 + m1^2 * v22 - 2 * m1 * m2 * v12, 0)) / r2 draw_torus(dat2, dir_hat, phig,            lo = dir_hat - 1.96 * se_dir, hi = dir_hat + 1.96 * se_dir)

what: Same model, same numbers, in Python
title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: model class mgcv twin pycircstat2’s CLRegression(..., family=vmlss); two implementations differentially tested every release — identical data, Newton-REML sides — agree machine precision cyclic-spline models like one (see tests/testthat/test-vmlss-parity.R dev/parity/ repository).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ziwei Huang. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Huang Z (2026). circlss: Circular-Response Location-Scale Families 'mgcv'. R package version 0.0.2, https://huangziwei.github.io/circlss/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {circlss: Circular-Response Location-Scale Families for 'mgcv'},   author = {Ziwei Huang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.2},   url = {https://huangziwei.github.io/circlss/}, }

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: Circular-response location-scale families mgcv’s GAM engine: fit penalized-spline regression models response angle, every distribution parameter getting smooth.  circular–circular fit natural canvas: predictor angle around ring, response angle around tube, fitted mean direction mu(phi) (red) winding surface — gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs="cc"), ~ s(phi, bs="cc")), family = vmlss()). Code torus article. Documentation: https://huangziwei.github.io/circlss/ Two families far: von Mises vmlss() (tan-half location link, log concentration — reference implementation) projected normal pnlss() (identity-linked Cartesian mean components — branch cut, fitted direction can sweep wind around full circle, natural choice circular–circular regression). remaining circular families (wrapped Cauchy, Jones–Pewsey, sine-skewed JP, cardioid, Cartwright, wrapped normal, Kato–Jones) follow v0.1.0, ported pycircstat2 recipe.

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: Requires mgcv >= 1.9.4 (R >= 4.5 recommended).
code: # released versions: not yet on CRAN; install from GitHub remotes::install_github("huangziwei/circlss")  # or from a local checkout install.packages("path/to/circlss", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: vmlss() mgcv general family: pass gam() list two formulas, one per distribution parameter — mean direction (Fisher–Lee tan-half link) log concentration. response radians, branch ([0, 2pi) (-pi, pi]). tan-half location link maps onto (-pi, pi): linear predictor’s intercept sets reference direction antipode unrepresentable (standard Fisher–Lee caveat). Log-likelihood derivatives implemented fourth order, full Newton REML works (optimizer = "efs" general-family criteria); everything mgcv provides general families — summary, plot, predict, gam.check, AIC, smooth selection, tensor smooths, random effects — comes free.
code: library(mgcv) library(circlss)  # distributional von Mises: mu and log-kappa each smoothed, REML selection b <- gam(list(theta ~ s(x),    # location, tan-half link                     ~ s(x)),   # log concentration          family = vmlss(), data = dat, method = "REML")  summary(b) plot(b, pages = 1) predict(b, newdata = nd, type = "response")  # columns: mu (radians), kappa  # circular covariate: mgcv's cyclic splines are native and correct b2 <- gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc"), ~ s(phi, bs = "cc")),           family = vmlss(), data = dat, method = "REML",           knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi)))

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: derivative algebra transcribed pycircstat2’s FD-verified implementations, every release differentially tested : models data (file-exchanged, Newton-REML sides) must agree within pinned tolerances. v0.0.1, battery agrees machine precision (~1e-11) parametric cyclic-spline models, ~3e-5 (smoothing parameters, log-likelihood) fitted curves within 2e-6 radians thin-plate models, basis eigen-decomposition makes bitwise agreement impossible. REML criteria match ~1e-9 normalizing conventions (hea reports -2 log REML, mgcv -log REML). dev/parity/run.sh reruns whole side--side comparison; frozen battery asserted hermetically tests/testthat/test-vmlss-parity.R.

what: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: general family implementing distributional regression circular (angular) response \(y\) radians projected normal law: \(y\) angle bivariate normal mean \((\mu_1, \mu_2)\) identity covariance. mean component gets linear predictor identity link, mgcv::gam called list two formulas: first names response models \(\mu_1\), second models \(\mu_2\).

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: link Two-element list link names two Cartesian mean components. "identity" available .

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: object class c("general.family", "extended.family", "family") use gam.

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: fitted mean direction \(\mathrm{atan2}(\mu_2, \mu_1)\) implied concentration grows \(\|\mu\|\). direction assembled two unconstrained components, link branch cut: unlike tan-half parameterization vmlss, fitted mean direction can cross angle can wind around circle (e.g. \(\mu(\varphi) = \varphi\) cyclic covariate), makes pnlss natural family circular-circular regression rotation-type association. trade-interpretability: location concentration entangled \((\mu_1, \mu_2)\) rather separated distinct parameters. Fitted values predict(..., type = "response") return two Cartesian components \((\mu_1, \mu_2)\) columns, matching identity links; compute direction atan2(fit[, 2], fit[, 1]) concentration scale sqrt(rowSums(fit^2)). Log-likelihood derivatives implemented fourth order (full Newton method = "REML", optimizer = "efs"). derivative algebra transcribed finite-difference-verified implementations Python package pycircstat2, fitted results differentially tested . "pearson" residuals alias "deviance" (signed root twice log-likelihood gap fitted-direction mode), pycircstat2.

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: Presnell, B., Morrison, S. P. Littell, R. C. (1998) Projected multivariate linear models directional data. Journal American Statistical Association 93, 1068-1077. Wood, S. N., Pya, N. Saefken, B. (2016) Smoothing parameter model selection general smooth models. Journal American Statistical Association 111, 1548-1575.

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
code: library(mgcv) #> Loading required package: nlme #> This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'. set.seed(1) n <- 300 x <- runif(n) m1 <- 1.5 * sin(2 * pi * x) + 0.5 m2 <- 1.2 * cos(2 * pi * x) y <- atan2(m2 + rnorm(n), m1 + rnorm(n))  # exact projected normal draws b <- gam(list(y ~ s(x), ~ s(x)), family = pnlss(), method = "REML") summary(b) #>  #> Family: pnlss  #> Link function: identity identity  #>  #> Formula: #> y ~ s(x) #> <environment: 0x562ce0127b40> #> ~s(x) #> <environment: 0x562ce0127b40> #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)    0.51074    0.07397   6.905 5.03e-12 *** #> (Intercept).1 -0.05383    0.06932  -0.777    0.437     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>          edf Ref.df Chi.sq p-value     #> s(x)   5.455  6.598  200.3  <2e-16 *** #> s.1(x) 4.522  5.566  146.2  <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 47.2% #> -REML =  383.6  Scale est. = 1         n = 300 fv <- fitted(b) direction <- atan2(fv[, 2], fv[, 1])

what: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: general family implementing distributional regression circular (angular) response \(y\) radians, $$y \sim \mathrm{vM}(\mu, \kappa),$$ mean direction \(\mu\) (log) concentration \(\kappa\) get linear predictor, may contain smooth terms. Used mgcv::gam list two formulas: first specifies response model \(\mu\), second model \(\log\kappa\).

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: link Two-element list link names, mean direction concentration. Currently defaults available: "tanhalf" location "log" concentration.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: object class c("general.family", "extended.family", "family") use gam.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: mean direction uses Fisher-Lee tan-half link, \(\mu = 2\arctan(\eta) \(-\pi, \pi)\): linear predictor's intercept sets reference direction antipode direction unrepresentable, standard caveat link. concentration uses log link. Log-likelihood derivatives fourth order implemented, family supports full Newton smoothing-parameter estimation method = "REML" (general-family criteria, including optimizer = "efs"). derivative implementations transcriptions finite-difference verified implementations Python package pycircstat2, fitted results differentially tested . response supplied radians; branch (example \([0, 2\pi)\) \((-\pi, \pi]\)) acceptable since density periodic. Fitted values two-column matrix: mean direction \((-\pi, \pi)\) concentration.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: Fisher, N. . Lee, . J. (1992) Regression models angular response. Biometrics 48, 665-677. Wood, S. N., Pya, N. Saefken, B. (2016) Smoothing parameter model selection general smooth models. Journal American Statistical Association 111, 1548-1575.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
code: library(mgcv) set.seed(1) n <- 300 x <- runif(n) mu <- 2 * atan(1.5 * sin(2 * pi * x)) kappa <- exp(1 + 0.8 * cos(2 * pi * x)) ## von Mises deviates via wrapped rejection-free approximation for the ## example only: use circular::rvonmises or the family's rd in practice y <- mu + rnorm(n) / sqrt(kappa)  # high-kappa approximation, example only b <- gam(list(y ~ s(x), ~ s(x)), family = vmlss(), method = "REML") summary(b) #>  #> Family: vmlss  #> Link function: tanhalf log  #>  #> Formula: #> y ~ s(x) #> <environment: 0x562cdd8baf10> #> ~s(x) #> <environment: 0x562cdd8baf10> #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)    0.10346    0.04183   2.474   0.0134 *   #> (Intercept).1  1.16066    0.07234  16.045   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>         edf Ref.df Chi.sq  p-value     #> s(x)   6.91  7.962 669.76  < 2e-16 *** #> s.1(x) 2.87  3.569  19.84 0.000442 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 60.7% #> -REML = 312.05  Scale est. = 1         n = 300 plot(b, pages = 1)

title: circlss 0.0.2
text: pnlss(): projected normal family — response angle bivariate normal mean (mu1, mu2) identity covariance; Cartesian mean components carry identity links linear predictors. tan-half branch cut: fitted mean direction can cross angle can wind around circle, making pnlss natural family circular–circular regression rotation-type association (theta ~ phi). Full fourth-order derivatives (Newton REML EFS). differential battery pycircstat2 doubles eight cases: four pnlss cases (parametric, thin plate, cyclic--winding, small-n) agree tolerance classes vmlss — near machine precision deterministic bases (winding cyclic case ~1e-10), optimizer-stopping tolerance thin-plate ones. TIGHT class’s coefficient/curve thresholds recalibrated 1e-9 1e-7 cover flatter parametric projected-normal likelihood (log-likelihood still agrees 1e-13 ).

title: circlss 0.0.1
text: Initial release. vmlss(): von Mises location-scale general family mgcv::gam() — Fisher–Lee tan-half link mean direction log link concentration, parameter linear predictor smooths. Log-likelihood derivatives fourth order, full Newton method = "REML" works (optimizer = "efs"). Differentially tested pycircstat2: identical models identical data (Newton-REML sides) agree machine precision parametric cyclic-spline fits, optimizer stopping tolerance (~3e-5, fitted curves within 2e-6 radians) thin-plate fits. Tolerances pinned test suite. derivative algebra transcribed pycircstat2’s finite-difference-verified implementations, R-side FD checks link chain assembled gradient/Hessian.
